title: AI File Envelope v1
description: Standard envelope describing target repository, path, schemas, and content for AI-generated files in the Horror$Place VM-constellation. Enforces schema-first, repo-role-aligned, implication-only authoring across all constellation repositories.

ai_file_envelope.description: Wrapper containing metadata and content for a single AI-generated artifact.

target_repo.description: Logical repository name in the VM-constellation. Must match one of the canonical repo identifiers.

target_path.description: Repository-relative file path; must be GitHub-compatible and match canonical directory conventions for the target repo.

schemaref.description: Canonical schema URI for JSON/NDJSON content; null for free-form text, code, or Markdown files. Must reference a schema defined in Horror.Place or HorrorPlace-Constellation-Contracts.

tier.description: Constellation tier classification for the artifact, determining visibility and governance constraints.

intended_audience.description: Primary audience for the artifact; informs CI validation strictness and documentation tone.

description.description: Concise summary of the artifact's purpose and alignment with constellation doctrine.

cross_repo_links.description: Explicit cross-repository references required by this artifact. Used for dependency tracking and CI validation.

type.description: Semantic type of the cross-repo link (e.g., invariant_bundle, seed_file, schema, deadledger_ref, registry_entry).

path.description: Relative path to the referenced artifact within target_repo.

content_language.description: Language/format of the body content; determines validation and linting rules.

invariants_used.description: List of canonical invariant keys referenced by this artifact. Must match keys from the Horror.Place invariants schema.

metrics_used.description: List of canonical entertainment metric keys referenced by this artifact.

deadledger_surface.description: List of Dead-Ledger surface types referenced (e.g., for high-intensity or restricted artifacts).

body.description: Actual file content, structured according to content_language. For JSON/NDJSON, this is the parsed object; for text/code, it is an object with a 'text' field.

oneOf.description: Structured content for JSON/NDJSON files. Must validate against schemaref if non-null.
Text-based files (Markdown, code, config). Content goes in the 'text' field.

text.description: Raw file content; must be GitHub-ready with no inline citations or tool markers.
